title: Distinguish Whether An AGV Is In The System Versus In The Map
summary: Use the map monitor summary area and AGV map display to determine whether an AGV is currently in the viewed map, disconnected but still present in the map, or still part of the RMS system after being removed from the map.
when_to_use: Use when you need to interpret AGV presence and status in RMS/map views and determine whether an AGV is still shown in the active map, disconnected within the map, or present in the system but not associated with the viewed map.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to remove, repair, or recover an AGV.
Do not use this runbook to infer corrective actions beyond status interpretation.
Do not use this runbook to decide that an AGV has been intentionally removed unless the displayed counts and map state support that interpretation.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the map monitor summary area and AGV map display, interpret the displayed counts and AGV color/state, and record whether the AGV is in the map, disconnected in the map, or in the system but not in the map.

safety_notes: This source describes status interpretation only, not AGV control or recovery actions.
Do not infer removal or repair actions because the source only explains status meaning and count behavior.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the map monitor summary area
Access to the AGV map display

access_required: Access to the RMS/map monitor view that shows AGV summary counts
Access to the map view where AGVs are displayed
system_preconditions: The relevant map view must be open
The AGV summary area must be visible

visual_references.description: Map monitor summary area showing AGV-related indicators including disconnected AGV count and related summary fields.
Robot selection overview showing that purple means robot disconnected.

steps.title: Locate AGV summary counts
steps.purpose: Identify the summary area used to compare AGVs shown in the map versus AGVs still present in the system.
steps.instruction: Open the map monitor view and locate the summary area that shows AGV-related counts, including the indicators used to understand AGVs in the map and disconnected AGVs.
steps.expected_result: The AGV summary area is visible and ready for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The summary area is visible and AGV-related indicators can be read.
steps.failure_condition: The summary area is not visible or the AGV-related indicators cannot be identified.

actions.label: Open summary area
actions.exact_action: Locate the summary counts that show AGVs in the map and AGVs that are in the system but not added to the map.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map monitor summary area with AGV-related indicators, including disconnected AGV count and other summary fields.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed counts needed for interpretation are not available in the visible interface.

source_evidence.text: Locate the summary counts that show AGVs in the map and AGVs that are in the system but not added to the map.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The artifact covers the map monitor summary area and explains ... count of disconnected AGVs ...

steps.title: Compare map and system presence counts
steps.purpose: Determine whether the AGV population shown in the map differs from the AGV population still present in the system.
steps.instruction: Compare the count of AGVs shown in the map with the count of AGVs that are still in the system but not in the map.
steps.expected_result: You can tell whether there are AGVs present in the system that are not currently associated with the viewed map.
steps.healthy_condition: The counts align with a clear interpretation of map presence versus system-only presence.
steps.failure_condition: The counts do not support a clear interpretation or appear inconsistent with the map view.

actions.label: Compare counts
actions.exact_action: Compare the count of AGVs shown in the map with the count of AGVs that are still in the system but not in the map.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Summary indicators used to compare AGV presence in the map versus AGV-related system counts.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed counts and map state do not align with the source-described behavior.

source_evidence.text: Compare the count of AGVs shown in the map with the count of AGVs that are still in the system but not in the map.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You can have AGVs in the system, but not in the map...

steps.title: Check whether the specific AGV still appears on the map
steps.purpose: Determine whether the AGV remains associated with the viewed map.
steps.instruction: If checking a specific AGV, inspect the map and verify whether that AGV still appears on the map even if it is disconnected.
steps.expected_result: You can confirm whether the AGV is still displayed on the map.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is either visibly present on the map or clearly absent from the viewed map.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be located and the display does not provide enough information to determine whether it is still associated with the map.

actions.label: Inspect AGV on map
actions.exact_action: If checking a specific AGV on the map, observe whether it still appears on the map while disconnected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall map view where AGV presence or absence can be checked.
Robot selection/map-related view showing robot color meanings, including purple for disconnected.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the AGV cannot be located and the counts do not clarify whether it is still in the map.

source_evidence.text: If checking a specific AGV on the map, observe whether it still appears on the map while disconnected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: if you turn off the AGV, that AGV will still appear on the map...

steps.title: Interpret purple AGV as disconnected but still in the map
steps.purpose: Use the source-defined color meaning to distinguish a disconnected AGV from a removed AGV.
steps.instruction: If the AGV appears purple on the map or robot view, interpret that as disconnected while still remaining in the map.
steps.expected_result: A purple AGV is classified as disconnected but still in the map.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is visible and purple, matching the disconnected state described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV color/state does not match the source description or cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Interpret purple state
actions.exact_action: Interpret a purple AGV on the map as disconnected but still in the map, based on the source description.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot color legend/description indicating purple means robot disconnected.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the AGV appears in a state that conflicts with the source-described purple disconnected meaning.

source_evidence.text: Interpret a purple AGV on the map as disconnected but still in the map, based on the source description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: that AGV will still appear on the map, but as a purple AGV, that means it's disconnected and it's still in the map

steps.title: Interpret removed AGV as system-only and not in the viewed map
steps.purpose: Distinguish a removed AGV from a disconnected AGV that still appears in the map.
steps.instruction: If the AGV has been removed and is no longer associated with the map you are viewing, interpret it as still part of the overall system but not in that map.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is classified as system-only relative to the viewed map.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is not associated with the viewed map, while the source interpretation still treats it as part of the system.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV appears absent from the map but the available evidence does not support whether it remains part of the system.

actions.label: Interpret removed state
actions.exact_action: Interpret an AGV that has been removed as no longer associated with the map you are viewing, while still remaining part of the overall system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Summary/map context used to distinguish AGVs still in the system from AGVs associated with the viewed map.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed counts and map state do not align with the source-described behavior.

source_evidence.text: Interpret an AGV that has been removed as no longer associated with the map you are viewing, while still remaining part of the overall system.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: once you remove it, that AGV is no longer associated with the map that you're looking at, but it's still part of your system.

steps.title: Record the AGV status interpretation
steps.purpose: Capture the final interpretation for support or reference use.
steps.instruction: Record whether the AGV is in the map, disconnected in the map, or in the system but not in the map.
steps.expected_result: A clear status interpretation is documented.
steps.healthy_condition: One of the source-supported status outcomes is recorded without adding unsupported corrective actions.
steps.failure_condition: The status cannot be determined from the available map and summary evidence.

actions.label: Document status
actions.exact_action: Record whether the AGV is in the map, disconnected in the map, or in the system but not in the map.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the status cannot be determined from the displayed counts and map state.
Do not infer removal or repair actions because the source only explains status meaning and count behavior.

source_evidence.text: Record whether the AGV is in the map, disconnected in the map, or in the system but not in the map.
The user can distinguish between AGVs that are active in the map, disconnected but still shown in the map, and AGVs that remain in the system after being removed from the map.

success_criteria: The user can distinguish between an AGV that is in the map, disconnected but still shown in the map, and in the system but not in the map.
The interpretation is based on displayed counts and map state consistent with the source description.
healthy_conditions: A purple AGV is interpreted as disconnected and still in the map.
A removed AGV is interpreted as no longer associated with the viewed map but still part of the system.
failure_conditions: Displayed counts and map state do not align with the source-described behavior.
The AGV cannot be confidently classified as in-map, disconnected in-map, or system-only.
The interface does not provide enough visible information to support the interpretation.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed counts and map state do not align with the source-described behavior.
Escalate if the status cannot be determined from the available summary and map views.
Do not infer removal or repair actions because the source only explains status meaning and count behavior.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map monitor summary area with AGV-related indicators.
Robot color meaning showing purple as disconnected.
Overall map view for checking whether an AGV still appears on the map.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: You can have AGVs in the system, but not in the map... if you turn off the AGV, that AGV will still appear on the map, but as a purple AGV, that means it's disconnected and it's still in the map... once you remove it, that AGV is no longer associated with the map that you're looking at, but it's still part of your system.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide exact field labels for the map-versus-system counts.
The source packet does not provide a dedicated image showing the specific in-system-but-not-in-map count.
The source packet does not provide a formal recording location or template for documenting the interpreted status.
The source does not define follow-up actions after classification beyond escalation for inconsistency.
